hypothesis: Extending the closed-loop optimization framework to a multi-objective Bayesian optimization targeting reactor yield, long-term electrode stability, and membrane durability simultaneously will reveal that the operating conditions maximizing instantaneous reactor yield (1.5 M KHCO₃, 0.75 M K₂CO₃) systematically accelerate bipolar membrane degradation through localized pH gradients, such that the true optimum for time-integrated CO production per unit electrode area occurs at a lower bicarbonate concentration (approximately 0.8–1.0 M KHCO₃) where the trade-off between per-cycle reactor yield and component lifetime favors sustained performance over hundreds of operating hours.
